note: Examples only. Not enabled by default. CodeRail ships no active hooks. Review these, then copy the ones you want into your Claude Code settings or your git hooks. See references/HOOKS.md for what each does and when to use it.
_design_rule: Never wire blueprint_check into a blocking hook. It is an educational reminder layer (non-blocking by design). Only coordinate_check and drift_check are hard enough to gate commits.

PostToolUse._when: After any Write/Edit. Soft reminder to trace the change.

hooks.command: echo 'CodeRail: if this was a non-trivial change, write a trace event. Run: python3 scripts/trace_event.py --target . --type change --summary "..." --files "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" --task T-XXX'

Stop._when: At the end of an agent turn. Surfaces a short doctor summary so drift does not accumulate silently.

hooks.command: python3 scripts/doctor.py --target . 2>/dev/null | head -30 || echo '(doctor skipped: not a CodeRail project)'

pre_commit_examples._note: These are NOT Claude Code hooks. They go in .git/hooks/pre-commit (or your pre-commit framework) and gate commits. Copy the ones matching your risk tolerance.

coordinate_gate._what: Block commit if any active/doing/done task is missing G/V/P fields.
coordinate_gate.command: python3 scripts/coordinate_check.py --target . || (echo 'BLOCKED: coordinate_check failed — a task is missing G/V/P. Run /task-contract.' && exit 1)

drift_gate._what: Block commit if drift is detected (orphan tasks, done without verify trace, etc.).
drift_gate.command: python3 scripts/drift_check.py --target . || (echo 'BLOCKED: drift detected — resolve before commit. Run /drift-check.' && exit 1)

trace_index_refresh._what: Regenerate the trace index so it stays fresh (soft, never blocks).

do_not_wire: blueprint_check.py — educational only; blocking on missing diagrams would misfire on every new project and violates its non-judgmental design.
doctor.py as a commit gate — it is a read-only health report, not a gate. Its findings are warnings, not failures.
